evidence.scientific_clarity: Two-panel ROC+PRC with 4 classifiers, diagonal reference, prevalence baseline, and per-method AUROC/AP annotations fully satisfy the brief.
evidence.typography: pdffonts shows DejaVuSans Type 3 (bitmap) fonts; script never sets pdf.fonttype=42 or svg.fonttype=none, so text is not selectable/editable.
evidence.palette: Gray/blue/orange/red palette avoids green/red directional trap; line-style differentiation aids colorblind readers, but red+orange pairing can be problematic for deuteranopia.
evidence.layout_density: 9x4.2 figure with tight_layout(pad=1.5), axis limits trimmed to data range, no gaping whitespace; both panels are well-utilized.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption exactly matches figure values; legends placed sensibly (lower-right ROC, upper-right PRC); top/right spines removed; prevalence baseline labeled.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF embeds DejaVuSans as Type 3 (bitmap glyphs), not Type 42 (TrueType); text is not selectable; pdf.fonttype=42 was never set in the script.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (26 KB), figure.png (124 KB), figure.svg (76 KB), caption.tex, and gen_figure.py all present and non-trivial.
evidence.caption_quality: Structured caption with bold title, per-panel explanation, all four numerical AUROC and AP values explicitly stated, and data provenance cited.

overall_strengths: Complete, publication-quality figure that fully addresses the brief: correct curve types, reference lines, and per-method numeric annotations in legends and caption.
Caption is detailed and self-contained, reporting all eight metric values (AUROC and AP for every classifier) and citing the data source.
overall_weaknesses: Critical vector-fidelity failure: pdf.fonttype is not set to 42, resulting in Type 3 bitmap-embedded DejaVuSans fonts instead of selectable TrueType text in the PDF.
Palette uses the matplotlib default DejaVu Sans typeface (flagged by the rubric) and red+orange combination that can be indistinguishable for deuteranopic readers without the line-style cue.
